name: South Korea
summary: Critical endpoint of helium-semiconductor cascade. KOSPI deepened decline to 5,281.76 (-2.9% Monday, -16.3% monthly). Foreign capital flight accelerated: 51.4T won ($37.8B) sold since Feb — largest since GFC. Samsung foreign ownership at 48.9% (12.5-year low). Won at 1,512 (17-year low). Helium data conflict: Samsung claims 6 months stock but TrendForce says industry at ~6 days. SK hynix likely more vulnerable than Samsung.

signals.content: KOSPI crashed 6.5% triggering circuit breaker — worst single-day drop since COVID. Samsung Electronics fell 8.2%. SK Hynix fell 9.1%. Driven by oil spike + helium supply concerns + global risk-off. (Sources: yfinance, Bloomberg, Reuters)

KOSPI at 5,438.87 (-0.4% daily, -10.6% monthly). Two semiconductor fabs announced reduced output due to helium allocation restrictions following Ras Laffan damage. USD/KRW at 1,508 — weakest since 2022. South Korea's energy import dependence (~70% from Gulf) makes it acutely exposed to Hormuz disruption. (Sources: yfinance, industry reports)

KOSPI triple cascade confirmed: helium→semiconductor fabs (~40% of -10.6% monthly decline, ~4.2%), Google TurboQuant AI memory compression (~25%, ~2.7%), foreign capital flight (~35%, ~3.7%). Foreigners dumped 51.4 trillion won ($37.8B) since February — largest outflow since Global Financial Crisis. Samsung foreign ownership hit 48.9% — lowest since October 2013 (12.5-year low). Won at 1,508/USD — weakest since March 2009 (17-year low). TrendForce's March 24 'two-week clock' on helium inventories means ~8 days remain before Asian chip fab inventories exhaust at full production rates. The helium cascade path (war→Qatar→helium→semiconductors→KOSPI) validated by 3 independent sources (TrendForce, Nikkei Asia, Korea International Trade Association). (Sources: yfinance, TrendForce, Korea International Trade Association)

KOSPI deepened decline to 5,281.76 (-2.9% Monday, -16.3% monthly). Foreign capital flight accelerated: ₩51.4T ($37.8B) sold since Feb — largest since GFC. Samsung foreign ownership at 48.9% (12.5-year low). Won at ₩1,512 (17-year low). Helium data conflict: Samsung claims 6 months stock but TrendForce says industry at ~6 days. SK hynix likely more vulnerable than Samsung. (Sources: yfinance, Korea International Trade Association, TrendForce, Korea Plus)

edges.context: Samsung and SK Hynix depend on helium for semiconductor fabrication. Qatar supplies ~25% of global helium. Ras Laffan damage directly disrupts Korean chip production.
South Korea imports ~70% of energy from the Gulf. Oil above $110 adds massive import costs. Direct exposure to Hormuz disruption.
Majority of Korean energy imports transit Hormuz. Closure directly impacts energy security and industrial production.

trigger_points.condition: Samsung or SK Hynix announces production halt due to helium shortage
trigger_points.mechanism: Confirmed fab shutdown would cascade to global semiconductor supply, AI infrastructure, and tech valuations worldwide. Currently only 'reduced output' reported, not full halt.
